title: Farewell to Jean Woodward
summary: After 32 years at Harvard Kennedy School, Jean Woodward will retire at the end of June.
body: Jean Woodward, Assistant Director of the National Security Fellows Program, with former Director of National Intelligence James Clapper following a National Security Fellows Executive Program he taught at the Kennedy School in August 2018. (Photo by Bernadette Schoukroun) After 32 years at Harvard Kennedy School, Jean Woodward will retire at the end of June. Starting at Harvard Kennedy School in the spring of 1987, Woodward’s first position was with Executive Education’s National Security Program as a program coordinator and as a writing consultant/coordinator for the National Security Fellows. She worked on the inaugural, historic Executive Program for Soviet Officers in 1991, which brought Soviet general and flag officers to Harvard Kennedy School to study democracy-building. Woodward has worked with every group of Fellows since the class of 1987 and has administered the Program for 20 years, continuing in that position when the Program joined the Belfer Center last year. She maintains close relationships with the more than 600 NSF alumni and has been privileged, she says, to watch their careers flourish. “These outstanding officers and intelligence community officials,” she notes, “have gone on to be chiefs of services or agencies, general and flag officers, and leaders of non-profit organizations—devoting their lives to public service.” She has mentored hundreds of other students, fellows, and young people during her time at Harvard. Prior to Harvard, Woodward held positions in electoral field organization, curriculum publishing, and writing and administration in the field of popular music and studio recording. A native Bostonian, she studied anthropology and communications at Syracuse University, both of which she employed in her career working with the military. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs

result.main_content_en: Jean Woodward, Assistant Director of the National Security Fellows Program at Harvard Kennedy School, is retiring after 32 years of service. She began her tenure in 1987 as a program coordinator and writing consultant for the National Security Fellows. Her significant contributions include working on the historic 1991 Executive Program for Soviet Officers, which brought Soviet military leaders to study democracy. Woodward has administered the program for 20 years, maintaining close ties with over 600 alumni who have advanced to top roles in the military, intelligence, and non-profit sectors. Throughout her career, she has mentored numerous students and fellows. Prior to Harvard, her background included work in electoral field organization, curriculum publishing, and the popular music industry. She holds a degree in anthropology and communications from Syracuse University.
result.main_content_zh: 哈佛大学肯尼迪学院国家安全研究员项目助理主任让·伍德沃德在服务32年后即将退休。她于1987年春季加入学院，最初担任国家安全研究员项目的项目协调员和写作顾问。她的重要贡献包括参与了1991年具有历史意义的苏联军官高管项目，该项目将苏联将官带到肯尼迪学院学习民主建设。伍德沃德管理该项目长达20年，并与600多名项目校友保持着密切联系，这些校友后来成为各军种或机构负责人、将官以及非营利组织的领导者。在哈佛期间，她还指导了数百名其他学生、研究员和年轻人。在加入哈佛之前，她曾在选举实地组织、课程出版以及流行音乐和录音室录制领域从事写作和管理工作。作为波士顿本地人，她在雪城大学学习人类学和传播学，并将这些知识应用于与军方合作的职业生涯中。
